L1.area: Admin dashboard UI components for rendering and displaying stored messages and execution results

L2.description: A stored cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where user-submitted message content is rendered in the admin interface without sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript via crafted HTML payloads that execute in the context of the administrator's browser session when messages are viewed, enabling credential theft from client-side storage.

L3.area: UI components responsible for rendering HTML message content and execution artifacts in the admin workflow and chat views
L3.description: A stored cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where user-submitted message content containing HTML, such as iframe elements with inline scripts, is rendered in the admin interface without sanitization using React's unsafe HTML rendering mechanism. When an administrator views stored messages or execution artifacts, the injected scripts execute in their browser context, enabling exfiltration of credentials from client-side storage.

ground_truth.reason: User-submitted chat messages containing arbitrary HTML are rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization in multiple admin UI components, allowing stored XSS that executes in the administrator's browser session.
